This is a line graph showing the trend of average heart rates over six months. The horizontal axis represents the months from January to June. The vertical axis displays the average heart rate in beats per minute, ranging from 70 to 75. 

The graph shows a generally increasing trend in average heart rate. Starting at around 70 beats per minute in January, the heart rate dips slightly in February to about 71, then decreases further in March to around 70. From March onward, the heart rate steadily increases, reaching approximately 72 in April, 74 in May, and finally reaching about 75 beats per minute in June. Overall, there's a clear upward trend in average heart rate from January to June.
